heroDescription.processed: Nestled on 85 wooded acres with a private 40-acre lake, Pinecrest offers the perfect escape into nature. Whether you prefer tent camping, RV sites with full hookups, or cozy cabins, we have a spot waiting for you.

ctaDescription.processed: Reserve your favorite campsite online or call our friendly office staff. Early booking is recommended for peak summer weekends and holiday periods.
ctaDescription.summary: Reserve your favorite campsite online or call our friendly office staff. Early booking is recommended for peak summer weekends and holiday periods.

body.processed:
Our premium lakefront RV sites offer the best views at Pinecrest. Wake up to morning mist rising from the lake, fish right from your site, and enjoy stunning sunsets from your campfire ring. These spacious pull-through sites accommodate rigs up to 45 feet and include full hookups with 50-amp service.

Site Details

Sites L1 through L15 are arranged along the north shore of Pinecrest Lake, each with a private lake view. Concrete pads, a picnic table, fire ring, and mature shade trees are standard at every site.
body.summary: Our premium lakefront RV sites offer the best views at Pinecrest. Wake up to morning mist rising from the lake, fish right from your site, and enjoy stunning sunsets from your campfire ring. These spa

image.alt: RV campsite with lake view at sunset

body.processed:
Our woodland tent sites are tucked among towering white pines and hardwoods, offering a true back-to-nature experience with plenty of privacy between sites. Each site includes a level tent pad, fire ring, picnic table, and bear-proof food storage locker.

Site Details

Sites W1 through W30 are spread along shaded forest paths, most within a 5-minute walk of the central bathhouse. Water spigots are located at convenient intervals throughout the tent camping area.
body.summary: Our woodland tent sites are tucked among towering white pines and hardwoods, offering a true back-to-nature experience with plenty of privacy between sites. Each site includes a level tent pad, fire r

image.alt: Tent pitched among tall pine trees in a wooded campsite

body.processed:
Our charming rustic cabins offer a step up from tent camping while keeping you close to nature. Each cabin sleeps up to six with a double bed, bunk beds, a small porch, and electricity. Bring your own bedding and cooking supplies, or add a linen package when you book.

Cabin Features

Eight cabins (C1-C8) are clustered near the lake with easy access to the swimming beach and boat launch. Each cabin has a covered porch with Adirondack chairs, an outdoor fire ring, and a picnic table.
body.summary: Our charming rustic cabins offer a step up from tent camping while keeping you close to nature. Each cabin sleeps up to six with a double bed, bunk beds, a small porch, and electricity. Bring your own

image.alt: Rustic wooden cabin nestled among pine trees

body.processed:
Experience camping in luxury with our safari-style glamping tents. Each canvas tent is furnished with a queen bed, nightstands, solar-powered string lights, and a private deck with valley views. Fresh linens, towels, and a coffee maker are included.

Glamping Details

Our four glamping tents (G1-G4) are positioned on a hillside overlooking the valley, providing maximum privacy and spectacular sunrise views. Each tent sits on a raised wooden platform with a private deck.
body.summary: Experience camping in luxury with our safari-style glamping tents. Each canvas tent is furnished with a queen bed, nightstands, solar-powered string lights, and a private deck with valley views. Fresh

image.alt: Luxury glamping tent with comfortable furnishings and string lights

entity.title: Heated Swimming Pool & Splash Pad

body.processed:
Cool off in our heated outdoor swimming pool or let the kids play at the adjacent splash pad. The pool area includes comfortable lounge chairs, shade umbrellas, and a poolside snack bar serving ice cream, drinks, and light snacks.

Pool Rules

  • Children under 12 must be accompanied by an adult
  • No glass containers in the pool area
  • Swim at your own risk - no lifeguard on duty
  • Pool towels not provided, please bring your own
body.summary: Cool off in our heated outdoor swimming pool or let the kids play at the adjacent splash pad. The pool area includes comfortable lounge chairs, shade umbrellas, and a poolside snack bar serving ice cr

entity.locationOnProperty: Central area, near the General Store
entity.hours: 10:00 AM - 8:00 PM daily (Memorial Day to Labor Day)

image.alt: Outdoor swimming pool surrounded by trees

entity.title: Camp General Store

body.processed:
Forgot the marshmallows? Our well-stocked camp store has everything you need for a great camping trip, from firewood and ice to groceries, camping supplies, and souvenirs. We also carry local products including honey, maple syrup, and homemade jams.

What We Carry

  • Firewood, kindling, and fire starters
  • Ice, beverages, and snacks
  • Basic groceries and canned goods
  • Camping gear and supplies
  • Fishing tackle and bait
  • Souvenirs and local products
body.summary: Forgot the marshmallows? Our well-stocked camp store has everything you need for a great camping trip, from firewood and ice to groceries, camping supplies, and souvenirs. We also carry local products

entity.locationOnProperty: Main entrance area, next to the check-in office

image.alt: Rustic camp general store with outdoor display

entity.title: Modern Bathhouse & Laundry

body.processed:
Our recently renovated bathhouse features private shower stalls, accessible restrooms, and a coin-operated laundry facility. The bathhouse is cleaned three times daily to maintain high standards of hygiene and comfort.

Facilities

  • 12 private shower stalls with hot water
  • Accessible restroom stalls
  • 6 coin-operated washers and dryers
  • Baby changing stations
  • Hair dryer stations
body.summary: Our recently renovated bathhouse features private shower stalls, accessible restrooms, and a coin-operated laundry facility. The bathhouse is cleaned three times daily to maintain high standards of hy

entity.locationOnProperty: Central campground, between tent and RV areas

body.processed:
Explore 12 miles of well-maintained hiking trails winding through pine forests, along the lakeshore, and up to scenic overlooks with panoramic views of the surrounding countryside. Trail maps are available at the camp office and posted at all trailheads.

Trail Guide

  • Lakeshore Loop (1.5 miles, Easy) - Flat path circling Pinecrest Lake, perfect for families
  • Pine Ridge Trail (3 miles, Moderate) - Climb through mature pine forest to the ridge overlook
  • Deer Hollow Trail (4 miles, Moderate) - Follow the creek through a scenic hollow
  • Summit Trail (3.5 miles, Challenging) - Steep climb to the highest point with 360-degree views
body.summary: Explore 12 miles of well-maintained hiking trails winding through pine forests, along the lakeshore, and up to scenic overlooks with panoramic views of the surrounding countryside. Trail maps are avai

image.alt: Hikers on a forested trail through tall pine trees

body.processed:
Pinecrest Lake is stocked annually with rainbow trout, largemouth bass, and bluegill, making it a fantastic fishing destination for anglers of all skill levels. Fish from the shore, the fishing dock, or rent one of our rowboats or canoes for an on-the-water experience.

Fishing Information

  • State fishing license required for ages 16+
  • Catch-and-release encouraged for bass over 18 inches
  • Rowboat and canoe rentals available at the dock
  • Fishing tackle and bait available at the General Store
  • Kids' fishing clinics held Saturday mornings in summer
body.summary: Pinecrest Lake is stocked annually with rainbow trout, largemouth bass, and bluegill, making it a fantastic fishing destination for anglers of all skill levels. Fish from the shore, the fishing dock,

image.alt: Person fishing from a dock on a calm lake at sunrise

body.processed:
Paddle across the calm waters of Pinecrest Lake or explore the scenic Cedar Creek, which flows from the lake through two miles of wetlands rich with wildlife. Single and tandem kayaks, canoes, and stand-up paddleboards are available for rent by the hour or day.

Rental Rates

  • Single kayak: $15/hour, $45/day
  • Tandem kayak: $25/hour, $65/day
  • Canoe: $20/hour, $55/day
  • Stand-up paddleboard: $20/hour, $50/day

Life jackets are provided with every rental. A brief orientation is required for first-time paddlers.
body.summary: Paddle across the calm waters of Pinecrest Lake or explore the scenic Cedar Creek, which flows from the lake through two miles of wetlands rich with wildlife. Single and tandem kayaks, canoes, and sta

image.alt: Kayakers paddling on a calm forest lake

body.processed:
The Mitchell family has been welcoming campers to Pinecrest since 1978. What started as a small tent camping area with a handful of sites has grown into an 85-acre, full-service campground and RV park while maintaining the family-friendly atmosphere and natural beauty that has kept guests coming back for generations.

Our Philosophy

We believe in providing a place where families can unplug from technology and reconnect with nature and each other. Our campground is designed to offer modern comforts without losing the authentic outdoor experience.

Sustainability

Pinecrest is committed to environmental stewardship. We use solar power for common area lighting, maintain a composting program, and have planted over 500 native trees in the past decade to enhance wildlife habitat.
body.summary: The Mitchell family has been welcoming campers to Pinecrest since 1978. What started as a small tent camping area with a handful of sites has grown into an 85-acre, full-service campground and RV park

body.processed:
To ensure all guests have an enjoyable and safe experience, please observe the following campground rules.

Quiet Hours

Quiet hours are from 10:00 PM to 7:00 AM. Please be respectful of fellow campers during these hours.

Check-In / Check-Out

Check-in time is 2:00 PM. Check-out time is 11:00 AM. Early check-in may be available upon request, subject to availability.

Pets

Well-behaved pets are welcome at most campsites. Dogs must be leashed at all times and never left unattended. Please clean up after your pet. Pets are not permitted in cabins, the pool area, or the beach.

Fires

Campfires are permitted only in designated fire rings. Use only firewood purchased from our camp store (bringing outside firewood is prohibited to prevent invasive species). Fires must be fully extinguished before leaving your site or going to sleep.

Cancellation Policy

Full refund if cancelled 14+ days before arrival. 50% refund for cancellations 7-13 days before arrival. No refund for cancellations less than 7 days before arrival.
